text.tspan: Top-down poster of the current OPH particle-mass pipeline. Read from the input row downward: common inputs, branching logic, sector-specific theorem lanes, current prediction surfaces, and final particle outputs.
Orange task cards mark exactly where proof / closure is still missing. Green particle cards are the present end nodes emitted by the live results table.
This chart treats the OPH axioms as the common starting point.
They provide the conceptual constraints upstream of every lane
Live value in this snapshot: P =
1.63094. In the current code this is
log_dim_H = 1e+122 feeds the current neutrino estimate lane. loops = 4 and
hadron_profile = demo are snapshot settings used by the live report.
How to read the mass derivation chart
Start with OPH axioms plus the declared live scalar P and the extra input surface used by the neutrino estimate lane.
The chart then branches into sector lanes. Each lane uses the same reading order: implemented theorem / technique, missing proof tasks, current prediction surface, and final tracked particle
Status snapshot: 6 of 21 tracked rows currently sit above continuation / simulation status. The orange cards show exactly where the proof burden remains open.
The cleanest current rows: massless photon and gluon sectors.
The cleanest current rows: massless photon
Use the realized gauge/content branch. In plain English: OPH
says the force-carrier sectors for electromagnetism and
color stay massless at the structural level.
No open blocker in current ledger
No open blocker is attached to these two massless rows in
Symbol: gamma. Particle of light; the electromagnetic force
Symbol: g. Strong-force carrier. Free gluons are not seen
Reference: no direct free-particle mass measurement.
The D10 running family is now isolated as the exact single-P
core; after the active-selector fix, the next live mover is one
coherent shared scalar readout map beneath the pole/effective
The D10 running family is now isolated as the
active-selector fix, the next live mover is
one coherent shared scalar readout map
From P the code builds M_U, solves alpha_U from the pixel
constraint, gets the electroweak scale v, runs couplings to
mZ_run, and then emits the running-family observables. The
local active-readout selector now treats that full
running-family quintet as the honest D10 surface until
common readout certification flips; only then may the
quartet move together to one coherent pole/effective family.
The exact inner gate is now that delta_alpha, delta_kappa,
delta_rho, and delta_rW must share one source family, one
Separate decimal precision in P from D10 transport
Push a single-P exact D10 electroweak closure
Current W/Z prediction surface from the active D10
Symbol: W. Charged weak-force boson used in beta-decay-type
Symbol: Z. Neutral weak-force boson from the same
This branch sits on top of D10; the synchronized core is
explicit and the remaining mass-moving burden is now a rank-one
This branch sits on top of D10; the

Take the D10 substrate, impose the critical-surface
condition, then use the synchronized transport core to emit
Higgs/top outputs. The current shortest exactness route is
no longer two separate observable leftovers, but one common
Make the Higgs/top critical surface fully rigorous
Current D11 Higgs/top synchronized-core plus readout-kernel
Symbol: H. Higgs boson tied to the Standard Model
Symbol: t. Heaviest known quark. In the live table the main
Still a continuation branch; the newer theorem lane exists
Still a continuation branch; the newer
theorem lane exists locally but is not
The live predictor still uses the Stage-5 path: epsilon =
1/6, derived lepton exponents, and Koide-phase roots. The
local replacement lane now carries C_e_hat -> g_e -> Y_e
plus a shared charged-budget certificate route whose current
canonical decomposable charged family. The smallest current
live witness beneath that route is the explicit charged
mean witness closes against the proxy-supported min-gap
side, the existing sector equalizer and gluing norm should
collapse and the stored shared scale can promote.
Replace Koide-assisted lepton fitting with a
Symbol: e. Light charged matter particle found in atoms.
Symbol: mu. Heavier unstable cousin of the electron.
Symbol: tau. Heaviest charged lepton; a short-lived electron
Still a texture continuation branch, not a closed OPH flavor
Still a texture continuation branch, not a
The live predictor still uses Stage-5 integer textures,
epsilon = 1/6, and CKM-like mixing. The newer local lane now
emits a theorem-shaped transport kernel, a centered
then a projective same-label eigenline readout plus a common
sector-response object. The old hand-set odd quark weights
are gone: the local quark shape map is now a
coefficient-free single-kappa projector ray, and the quark
norm is already numerically pinned by the shared charged
scalarization candidate. The current mass-moving object is a
trace-zero quadratic family excitation evaluator on the
ordered branch-generator coordinate X_ord; that is the
smallest reduced family that can widen the two independent
quark-sector gaps without falling back to free three-weight
Bridge edge statistics to the Yukawa dictionary if
Derive quark textures, CKM, and CP from overlap
Current quark continuation surface. The tracked top row
lives in D11, not this column.
The live public flavor rows still use D6 capacity estimates,
but the sandbox now has a forward-usable selector-law neutrino
The live public flavor rows still use D6
capacity estimates, but the sandbox now has a
The current predictor still uses the D6 capacity estimate.
The local sandbox now closes the selector law under a
standard-math-fixed Hilbert-Schmidt pullback metric on the
isotropic branch and now carries a centered chordal/cosine
scalar-evaluator candidate at the actual equal-split
selector point, with the sharp intrinsic origin candidate
being the Hermitian displacement norm of a centered OPH
Majorana edge-character functor, and the local bundle now
exports actual eigenmasses, splittings, and normal-like
ordering under selector-law standard-math certification. The
next mass-moving object is the defect-weighted mu_e family
driven by a same-label overlap-defect weight normalizer;
that is the first local object that can lift the exact 1-2
near-degeneracy while keeping the centered selector and
Derive neutrino masses from screen capacity as a
Derive the Majorana phase pullback metric needed
Current public D6 flavor-row surface plus forward-usable
Symbol: nu_e. Extremely light neutral lepton of the electron
Symbol: nu_mu. Extremely light neutral lepton of the muon
Symbol: nu_tau. Extremely light neutral lepton of the tau
Explicitly simulation-dependent debug/demo outputs, not hadron closure.
Explicitly simulation-dependent debug/demo outputs, not hadron closure.
The current path steps down from D10 alpha_s to Lambda_MSbar^(3), then feeds the quenched demo lattice lane. These are bound-state
outputs, so this column combines quark-sector descendants with nonperturbative QCD simulation machinery.
Close the nonperturbative QCD hadron branch
Unquench hadron branch and publish systematics
Current demo hadron output surface. Useful for debugging, not for closure claims.
Symbol: p. Stable positively charged hadron found in atomic
Symbol: n. Neutral hadron found in atomic nuclei.
Symbol: pi0 proxy. Light meson proxy from the current demo
Symbol: rho(770)0 proxy. Short-lived vector-meson proxy from
calibration = produced by the current P-driven electroweak chain
secondary = quantitative branch built on an earlier calibrated
continuation = output exists, but theorem-level closure is still
simulation = bound-state / lattice-heavy lane, not clean closure
Lepton = electron-like matter particle; neutrinos are neutral
Quark = elementary constituent of hadrons such as protons and
RG running / matching = translating couplings and masses between
Quenched = simplified QCD simulation that omits some vacuum-quark
This chart shows the current implemented pipeline and its open
tasks. It is not a claim that the full particle zoo is already on
one finished theorem chain from P + axioms.
